posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Flat out gorgeous.

Pretty much everything that I want in a mature, old school Bordeaux.

Pulled cork for an hour before pouring, this beauty is at apogee. I have had this wine several times over its life, and this is its best showing for me.

Wide open aromatically. Gorgeous, warming, musky, smokey Bordeaux perfume. Silky palate with layers of red fruit and plummy darks, clearly from a warm vintage. Savoury herbs, greens, saline. Dried meats, saddle leather, old barn wood. Tannins fully resolved. Continued to open and show greater depth and character over the course of a 2-hour meals. The nose is beguiling, continuing to blossom further throughout. Outstanding.

(95 pts.)

posts.name: Jim Freeman
posts.cooked: I think mine are gone…could not wait as long as you. Thanks for the update.

posts.name: Corey Nøɤܝ©Ʞ
posts.cooked: Nice note Bobby. Sounds like a delicious treat.

posts.name: Marc Frontario
posts.cooked: Heaven…Sociando, a top ten Chateau for me…great consistency with their wines…and they scream bordeaux
Been wanting to open my '95, maybe I will wait a bit longer. Thanks for the note.

posts.name: Lee Short
posts.cooked: Thanks for note. I have one left, and was thinking that I should get around to opening it soon. Of course, I have a lot of wine that’s in that category.

posts.name: Soren R Nielsen
posts.cooked:
Great note.
Description ticks all My boxes.

Thanks, Søren.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Soren R Nielsen:

Great note.
Description ticks all My boxes.
One left in cellar. (and an '86 too)

Thanks, Søren.

I almost pulled the '86 instead (have an '83 remaining, as well), but wasnt sure exactly how it would show for a friend. The 1990 was a better choice, more accessible style given the vintage.

posts.name: john stimson
posts.cooked: I have about 1/2 case left. there was a lot of noise about prominent bell pepper in this wine, but that has never really bothered me much and was not really noticeable to me in the last couple of bottles.

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: The 1985 is very nice too!

posts.name: Pat_Martin
posts.cooked:
No, no, no…

These are green and lean, and as Jeff Leve will tell you, their yields are way too high to make good wine. And Clive Coates or some other terroirist would tell you that this isn’t a serious wine because it lacks a great terroir. A wine buddy of mine sums up this Chateau with “the wines taste like dirt”.

So please, move along, there’s nothing to see or buy here, just leave Sociando Mallet to us philistines who drink and treasure such wines in our ignorance.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Pat Martin:

So please, move along, there’s nothing to see or buy here, just leave Sociando Mallet to us philistines who drink and treasure such wines in our ignorance.

We are Country, when Country isn’t cool…

posts.cooked: Ignorant bliss Pat

posts.name: Arv R@()
posts.cooked: i think the 2003 is as good as the 1990. hard to believe they would ever duplicate that. sort of like Roger Maris’s freak year.

posts.name: David J
posts.cooked: A case of these just came up in the hdh auction. I wanted them, but I thought they were bid up a little high for me

posts.name: Pat_Martin
posts.cooked:
David J:

A case of these just came up in the hdh auction. I wanted them, but I thought they were bid up a little high for me

You see! This is what I mean. This used to be an under the radar wine. I’ve reloaded on it several times for $65-85 not all that long ago, but it’s now well north of $100.

posts.name: Robert Alfert
posts.cooked:
Arv R:

i think the 2003 is as good as the 1990. hard to believe they would ever duplicate that. sort of like Roger Maris’s freak year.

I’ve gone through 9 bottles of the 2003. I don’t think the '03 hits the same highs as the '90, the fruit is more ripe and acid is lower. It’s really good, like really good, but I think the '90 is great. Of course, I could be 100% wrong, and thus will sit on my remaining 3 bottles!

posts.cooked:
Their '89 is another good wine as well.
